title: Top Five Martin Scorsese Movies. Plus: Edward Norton! | The Big Picture
format: Ahead of The Irishman, Adam Nayman and Chris Ryan each run their Scorsese top five (Sean plays along too); then Sean sits down with Edward Norton about Motherless Brooklyn.

segments.heading: What Makes Scorsese, Scorsese
segments.note: before the lists, a conversation about his career

films.title: The Irishman
films.note: This weekend's Netflix release is the peg for the episode. The hosts save the movie itself for next week.

films.title: Mean Streets
films.note: Early example of Scorsese synthesizing genre from inside and outside American crime pictures: Italian melodrama grafted onto B-movie plotting.

films.title: The Godfather
films.note: Contrast point: Coppola was more overtly reinventing the gangster genre in the same era than Scorsese was.

films.title: Cape Fear
films.note: Chris's marker for when Scorsese stops being a crime-genre director in any strict sense. Before this, his real genre was New York City itself.

films.title: Network
films.note: Cited as a rare peer to Scorsese's The King of Comedy in predicting America's relationship to fame.

films.title: Joker
films.note: This year's Scorsese homage/riff, leaning on Taxi Driver and The King of Comedy.

films.title: After Hours
films.note: Adam crossed this off his list on the way to the studio in favor of The Last Waltz. It turns up on both Chris's and Sean's top fives anyway.

films.title: New York Stories
films.note: Chris's shoutout to the Nick Nolte segment 'Life Lessons' (not a full film of his own, but one of Scorsese's best hours). Skip the other two segments.

segments.heading: Adam Nayman's Top Five Scorsese Movies
segments.subject: Martin Scorsese

films.title: Goodfellas
films.note: No. 1. Unanimous pick across all three hosts.

films.title: Silence
films.note: No. 2. Adam calls it the best of Scorsese's late films. Isao Gata's performance is his favorite in any Scorsese movie.

films.title: The Age of Innocence
films.note: No. 3. Praised for the restraint in Daniel Day-Lewis and Michelle Pfeiffer's scenes together.

films.title: Taxi Driver
films.note: No. 4. Also Sean's No. 4.

films.title: The Last Waltz
films.note: No. 5. All three hosts had this at No. 5.

segments.heading: Chris Ryan's Top Five Scorsese Movies
segments.subject: Martin Scorsese

films.title: Goodfellas
films.note: No. 1. Unanimous pick across all three hosts.

films.title: The Color of Money
films.note: No. 2. Chris's favorite Newman-Cruise sequence: 'Werewolves of London.'

films.title: After Hours
films.note: No. 3. Also Sean's No. 3.

films.title: The Wolf of Wall Street
films.note: No. 4. Chris calls it the funniest movie Scorsese ever made.

films.title: The Last Waltz
films.note: No. 5. All three hosts had this at No. 5.

segments.heading: Sean Fennessey's Top Five Scorsese Movies
segments.subject: Martin Scorsese

films.title: Goodfellas
films.note: No. 1. Unanimous pick across all three hosts.

films.title: The King of Comedy
films.note: No. 2. Sean links it to Network in predicting America's relationship to fame.

films.title: After Hours
films.note: No. 3. Also Chris's No. 3.

films.title: Taxi Driver
films.note: No. 4. Also Adam's No. 4.

films.title: The Last Waltz
films.note: No. 5. All three hosts had this at No. 5.

films.title: Raging Bull
films.note: The big non-pick. Chris: 'the masterpiece that has never really worked for me'; Adam says it's never been super pleasurable to watch.

films.title: The Departed
films.note: Adam is on record disliking it; Chris says it's an important movie to him despite its flaws.

films.title: Shutter Island
films.note: Named as an example of late-period Scorsese nobody reached for.

films.title: Motherless Brooklyn
films.note: Norton's first film as director in 19 years: he wrote, directed, and stars, moving Jonathan Lethem's novel from present-day to 1950s New York.

films.title: Fight Club
films.note: What Norton was making when he first optioned Lethem's novel.

films.title: Keeping the Faith
films.note: Norton's actual directorial debut, made while Motherless Brooklyn sat in a drawer for years.

films.title: American History X
films.note: Where Norton met Toby Emmerich, the eventual Warner Bros. exec who championed Motherless Brooklyn for two decades.

films.title: Citizen Kane
films.note: Norton's touchstone for an actor directing his own passion project.

films.title: Reds
films.note: Warren Beatty's actor-directed epic, the other model Norton cites.

films.title: Unforgiven
films.note: Another actor-director passion project Norton points to.

films.title: Dances with Wolves
films.note: Same list: actor-directors who found the material that fit them.

films.title: Sweet Smell of Success
films.note: Someone pointed out to Norton that Motherless Brooklyn is set the same year as this film, and shares its vibe.

films.title: Chinatown
films.note: Norton's noir reference point for using genre to reveal how a city was really built.

films.title: The Big Sleep
films.note: Cited alongside Chinatown as the noir tradition Norton calls secretly patriotic.

films.title: Forrest Gump
films.note: Norton's example of the afflicted-underdog structure he used for Lionel, via screenwriter Eric Roth.

films.title: Good Will Hunting
films.note: Same lineage of underdog narratives Norton cites.

films.title: Rain Man
films.note: Also cited in that same lineage.

films.title: A Beautiful Mind
films.note: Rounds out Norton's list of films that make you root for someone because of an affliction, not despite it.

films.title: Anima
films.note: Norton's 'last great thing': Paul Thomas Anderson and Thom Yorke's short film for Netflix.

films.title: A Prophet
films.note: Jacques Audiard's prison drama. Norton ranks it with The Godfather and Goodfellas among the great gangster films.

films.title: The Beat That My Heart Skipped
films.note: Another Audiard film Norton recommends.

films.title: Rust and Bone
films.note: Audiard's film with Marion Cotillard, Norton's parting recommendation.

referenced.group: Scorsese's own Sight & Sound top 10 (he actually listed 12)

The Beat That My Heart Skipped.title: The Beat That My Heart Skipped
